postData.contentHtml:
In a FIFA World Cup Round of 16 match that will undoubtedly be etched into football history, Argentina delivered an astonishing comeback, defeating Egypt 3-2 after being down by two goals late in the game. This dramatic victory has not only thrilled fans worldwide but also caught the attention of sporting legends, including NFL icon Tom Brady.

Argentina found themselves in a precarious position, trailing Egypt 2-0 well into the second half. However, the South American giants refused to yield. The momentum began to shift in the 79th minute when Christian Romero found the back of the net, narrowing the deficit to 2-1. Just four minutes later, in the 83rd minute, the maestro Lionel Messi netted the equalizer, sending a wave of euphoria through the stadium. The incredible turnaround was completed in the 92nd minute of extra time when Enzo Fernandez scored the decisive goal, securing a breathtaking 3-2 victory for Argentina.

The sheer audacity and execution of Argentina's comeback prompted a remarkable reaction from Tom Brady. Known for leading arguably the greatest comeback in Super Bowl history—where his New England Patriots overcame a 28-3 deficit against the Atlanta Falcons—Brady took to Twitter/X to express his awe. His succinct tweet, "Yeah so that might top 28-3," immediately ignited discussions across social media platforms.

Brady's 28-3 Super Bowl LI comeback in 2017 remains a benchmark in professional sports for its improbability and magnitude. For the legendary quarterback to suggest that Argentina's recent triumph could potentially eclipse that monumental achievement speaks volumes about the intensity and drama witnessed in the Argentina-Egypt clash.

Social media has been abuzz with largely positive sentiment, though a spirited debate comparing the two historic comebacks is well underway. While Patriots fans may hold a special place for their team's Super Bowl heroics, there's no denying that Argentina's late-game surge, orchestrated by its star-studded lineup, has earned its place among the most unforgettable rallies in sports history.
postData.title: Tom Brady: Argentina's World Cup Comeback 'Might Top' Patriots' Legendary 28-3 Rally
postData.summary: NFL legend Tom Brady took to social media, suggesting Argentina's incredible 3-2 World Cup win over Egypt, coming from two goals down, might even surpass the New England Patriots' iconic 28-3 Super Bowl comeback.
